skill.name: localhero
skill.description: Guides AI coding assistants through Localhero.ai i18n workflows, including source strings, translation keys, glossary use, and CLI-driven translation updates.
skill.summary: Manage Localhero.ai source strings and translation workflows in code projects.
skill.author: localheroai

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are mostly markdown examples for the Localhero.ai CLI, not hidden executable code. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill allows Bash access to `npx @localheroai/cli *`, uses a network service, reads and writes project translation files, and documents API key authentication. No prompt injection, obfuscation, weak cryptography, or malicious exfiltration intent was found in the reviewed files.

medium_findings.title: Network-Enabled CLI Execution Is Allowed
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access to `npx @localheroai/cli *` and instructs agents to run Localhero.ai CLI commands. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can execute an external npm package, contact Localhero.ai, and modify project translation files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and workflow commands directly authorize Localhero CLI execution. The behavior is expected for an i18n CLI skill, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: API Key Handling Requires Care
medium_findings.description: The documentation includes non-interactive login with an API key and the LOCALHERO_API_KEY environment variable. Passing secrets on a command line can expose them through shell history or process inspection, so users should prefer environment or secret-manager workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key usage is documented explicitly and is a common authentication pattern. The risk is credible because command-line secrets can leak, but there is no evidence the skill exfiltrates credentials.

medium_findings.title: Translation Commands Can Modify Project Files
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to add or modify source locale keys and run commands that push, pull, clone, or translate files. This is expected functionality, but users should review diffs because generated translations and CLI operations may change repository content.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow and CLI reference clearly describe file-changing translation operations. This matches the product purpose, but it creates filesystem impact that should be disclosed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Product and Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network URL matches point to Localhero.ai, npm package documentation, Claude Code documentation, Cursor documentation, GitHub, and skills.sh. They are user-facing documentation or service links, not hidden endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in markdown documentation and align with the stated Localhero.ai translation workflow. No suspicious or unrelated network destination was found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Reconnaissance Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance lines are markdown text or table formatting, not cryptographic code or system probing logic. No evidence found for weak crypto usage or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows product description, gettext documentation, and environment variable table rows. These locations do not contain executable cryptographic or reconnaissance code.

low_findings.title: API Host Override Could Be Misused If Untrusted
low_findings.description: The CLI reference documents LOCALHERO_API_HOST for development. This is not malicious by itself, but users should not set it from untrusted instructions because it could redirect API traffic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The setting is explicitly documented as a development override, which is legitimate. The risk depends on user configuration and no misuse is shown in the skill files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad Bash Allowlist for Localhero CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed tool pattern permits any arguments under `npx @localheroai/cli *`. This supports normal workflows but increases blast radius if an agent is prompted to run destructive CLI options.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowlist and CLI options are directly visible. The concern is bounded to the Localhero CLI command namespace, not arbitrary shell execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command-Line Secret Argument Documented
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation shows `login --api-key <key>` as a non-interactive option. Secrets passed as process arguments can be exposed in logs, shell history, or process listings.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API key flag is directly documented. This is a recognized secret-handling risk, although the skill also documents an environment variable alternative.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent source strings without manually managing every translation file. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Localhero.ai i18n workflows using project configuration, glossary terms, and CLI commands.
actual_capabilities: Reads localhero.json guidance to identify source locale and translation file paths.
Helps add or modify source language strings using existing key conventions.
Uses Localhero.ai glossary and settings commands to match terminology and voice.
Guides translation generation with changed-only workflows when appropriate.
Explains when GitHub Actions can handle translations automatically.
Provides CLI references for push, pull, clone, glossary, settings, and login workflows.
limitations: Requires a project already configured for Localhero.ai or ready for initialization.
Requires Localhero.ai CLI authentication before network translation commands work.
Does not independently verify translation quality across every target language.
Can modify translation files, so repository diffs should be reviewed before commit.

use_cases.title: Add Product UI Copy
use_cases.description: Add new source strings for a feature while matching existing key names and product terminology.

use_cases.title: Prepare Translation Updates for a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Generate changed-only translations and keep the pull request diff focused on new or modified strings.

use_cases.title: Check Project Localization Settings
use_cases.description: Review glossary terms, tone, style, source locale, and target language settings before editing copy.

prompt_templates.title: Find Translation Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this project for Localhero.ai configuration. Tell me the source locale, translation file paths, and whether translations run through a GitHub Action.

prompt_templates.title: Add Source Strings
prompt_templates.prompt: Add source strings for this new UI flow. Follow the existing key naming pattern, use the project glossary, and only edit the source locale files.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Changed Translations
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current string changes and run the safest Localhero.ai workflow to generate only translations affected by this branch.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Localization Diff
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this localization diff for naming consistency, glossary compliance, accidental target-language edits, and risky CLI changes before I open a pull request.

output_examples.input: Add strings for a billing confirmation dialog.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the source locale file, adds namespaced billing keys, uses glossary-approved terms, and suggests generating changed-only translations.

output_examples.input: Check whether translations will run automatically on this pull request.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports whether a Localhero.ai GitHub Action exists and explains if manual CLI translation is needed.

output_examples.input: Review my localization changes before commit.
output: Confirms source strings match project tone.
Recommends reviewing generated target files before merge.

best_practices: Review localhero.json and existing source files before adding new keys.
Use changed-only translation commands for pull requests when the project supports them.
Keep API keys in approved secret storage and review generated file diffs.
anti_patterns: Do not edit target language files manually unless the project owner asks for it.
Do not create new key naming styles when existing conventions are present.
Do not run force, prune, clone, or host override commands without user approval.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps AI coding assistants manage source strings and Localhero.ai translation workflows in software projects.

faq.question: Does it translate text by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides the assistant to use Localhero.ai workflows and CLI commands for translation generation.

faq.question: Which files does it inspect?
faq.answer: It looks for localhero.json, source locale files, existing translation keys, glossary settings, and related workflow files.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. Localhero.ai CLI commands require login or an API key before they can access project translation services.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes, with review. Use changed-only workflows and inspect generated translation diffs before merging.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
